description: Member claims $847.50 purchase at Best Buy on 2/22 was unauthorized. However: member's device fingerprint matches in-store transaction device, member's Bluetooth was detected at that Best Buy location, and he has filed 4 previous disputed charges in 18 months — all at electronics retailers. Prior disputes totaled $3,200 and were all credited.

get_alert.monitoring_rule: RULE-CRD-021: Member dispute flagged for review — 3+ prior disputes within 24 months

get_member.fraud_risk_note: Elevated risk — 4 prior disputed charges in 18 months at electronics retailers. All previously credited. Pattern consistent with friendly fraud.

get_account.best_buy_history: Member has shopped Best Buy 7 times in past 12 months — all legitimate

disputed_transaction.location: 2020 E Little Creek Rd, Norfolk, VA
disputed_transaction.item_purchased: Samsung 65-inch QLED TV — confirmed by merchant receipt

get_triggering_activity.member_claim: Member states he was at work at Home Depot from 1:00 PM to 9:00 PM on 2/22 and did not make this purchase. Claims card was in his wallet the entire time.

digital_evidence_against_claim.device_fingerprint: Mobile banking app open on member's iPhone at 14:18 (4 min before transaction) from IP 72.11.44.18 — geolocation Best Buy Norfolk area
digital_evidence_against_claim.bluetooth_beacon: Member's phone Bluetooth MAC detected at Best Buy #841 Norfolk by store beacon network at 14:19 — 3 minutes before transaction
digital_evidence_against_claim.apple_pay_activity: Apple Pay notification sent to member's iPhone for $847.50 at 14:22:08 — push notification delivered to member's device
digital_evidence_against_claim.chip_read: EMV chip used — requires physical card presence. Not CNP, not tap, not fallback.

get_triggering_activity.employer_check: Home Depot punch records show member clocked out at 12:55 PM — 87 minutes before disputed transaction. Member was not at work during transaction.

get_compromise_assessment.likely_vector: none — transaction is consistent with member presence
get_compromise_assessment.evidence: EMV chip transaction requires physical card. Device fingerprint, Bluetooth beacon, Apple Pay notification, and employer records all contradict member's account. This pattern matches prior disputed transactions.
get_compromise_assessment.card_on_person: Member claims yes, but digital evidence inconsistent with claim

prior_alerts.investigation_notes: Black Friday purchase, member insisted card not present
Same Best Buy location. Credited after 10-day provisional — member never returned to discuss
Device fingerprint matched — denied. Member did not appeal.

get_prior_alerts.pattern_note: 5 disputes in 18 months, all electronics retailers, all card-present transactions, amounts $289-$1,350

get_reg_e_info.notification_deadline: Within 1 business day of determining unauthorized EFT
get_reg_e_info.provisional_credit_deadline: 10 business days from member notification (if determination incomplete)
get_reg_e_info.member_liability_if_authorized: Member is liable for authorized transactions — no credit due
get_reg_e_info.investigation_note: Evidence strongly suggests authorized transaction. Provisional credit should be withheld pending investigation. If denied, document clearly for potential appeal.

_meta.design_notes: Friendly fraud / first-party fraud. Member has history of disputing electronics purchases. Digital evidence (Bluetooth beacon, device fingerprint, Apple Pay notification, employer punch records) all contradict his claim. EMV chip rules out CNP or card-not-present. Tests: adversarial challenge path, deny_claim determination, handling of prior dispute pattern, low risk score despite clear evidence. Should result in deny_claim.

ground_truth.notes: Transaction was authorized by member. Friendly fraud pattern confirmed.
